core_action: A weathered brass industrial street lamp slowly warms from deep red to orange to amber before stabilizing.
A worn leather boot gently presses a raised wet wooden plank flat and continues walking.
Gray Harbor wakes: soup shop steam, lamp repair stall, coal cart, workers, wet cobblestones.
Qi Zhou walks naturally through the street carrying an old wooden-brass collection box, unnoticed.
Qi Zhou gradually slows near the old alley entrance while everyone else keeps moving.
Cold wind pushes old paper into a dark narrow alley; no people, only steam and silence.
A worker sits against the alley wall as if asleep; Qi Zhou notices from a respectful distance.
Qi Zhou gently adjusts the dead worker's sleeve and badge before starting work.
A worn wooden-and-brass dream collection box opens; an old silver needle rests inside.
Qi Zhou carefully brings the silver needle near the worker's ear with clinical restraint.
A thin white dream thread condenses into a clear crystal inside the box.
A faint blue trace appears inside the clear dream crystal, subtle and impossible.
Qi Zhou's hands stop for the first time; his expression remains controlled.
Distant footsteps approach the alley entrance while Qi Zhou closes the box.
Qi Zhou exits the alley with the box hidden close to his coat.
The 13th District dream collection station sits between pipes and old signage.
Rows of numbered wooden shelves hold old dream boxes, each repaired and labeled.
The old station worker opens the box and sees the faint blue trace.
The old man's hand trembles above the dream crystal.
A registration pen hovers above a blank line but never writes.
The old man locks the box below the counter instead of registering it.
The old man quietly warns Qi Zhou not to tell anyone today.
Qi Zhou leaves the station more quietly than he entered.
The open ledger shows one blank space where a record should be.
The station resumes normal rhythm, but the hidden box remains below the counter.
A cleaner, colder administrative office contrasts with Gray Harbor's damp streets.
A dull terminal flags a missing dream collection record.
A clerk dials an internal line after the anomaly persists.
Wen Xueheng receives the call while sorting an old sealed case file.
A thin file marked Case-13 lies briefly visible on Wen's desk.
Wen places a white pin on District 13 on a wall map.
The words 'missing registration' are written beside Qi Zhou's route.
Wen asks indirect questions near the soup shop while the owner protects Qi Zhou with silence.
The lamp repairman recalls the wind changing near the old alley.
Wen walks through the damp district with precise, restrained focus.
Qi Zhou sits alone on a gray river embankment under a pale sunless sky.
The wooden-brass box sits beside Qi Zhou on the damp concrete.
Qi Zhou opens the box again, careful and hesitant.
The blue trace inside the crystal is slightly deeper than before.
Qi Zhou reaches toward the crystal but stops before touching it.
An inspector enters the collection station with routine authority.
The old man calmly says the item was damaged and needs reprocessing.
Qi Zhou stands, hides the box inside his coat, and chooses not to return to the station.
Qi Zhou walks away from the station route and toward home.
Qi Zhou pulls the coat closed over the hidden box.
Gray Harbor's morning lamps glow as the city shifts into night routine.
Two patrol workers check documents on a narrow road.
Qi Zhou's hand touches the hidden box through his coat as he approaches patrol.
Qi Zhou passes the patrol without being stopped.
Only after passing does Qi Zhou release a small breath.
Wen writes Qi Zhou's name into her notebook under missing registration.
Wen writes: He lied.
A silent figure watches the collection station from across the street.
The hidden crystal remains under the station counter, faintly blue.
The old man looks at the crystal and whispers that it has returned.
Qi Zhou walks slowly across a wet bridge under industrial lamps.
Wen Xueheng approaches from the opposite end of the bridge.
They pass each other without stopping until Wen says his name.
Qi Zhou stops and slowly turns back.
Wen stands under a lamp, calm and unreadable.
They speak across distance; no one steps closer.
Wen's gaze briefly lands on the box and then moves away.
Qi Zhou lies badly, saying it may have been misremembered.
They walk away in opposite directions across the bridge.
Wen writes one line into her notebook.
Qi Zhou returns to a small worn room above an industrial street.
The small room is lit only by streetlamp glow through the window.
Water drops into an old iron basin under a roof leak.
Qi Zhou moves the basin slightly as if this happens every day.
The wooden-brass box is placed on the small table.
Qi Zhou opens the box in the quiet room.
The blue inside the crystal is stronger than before but still not glowing like a special effect.
A small radio emits a brief distortion and returns to normal.
Qi Zhou looks toward the radio, then back to the box.
Qi Zhou hides the box at home instead of returning it.
A streetlamp outside flickers softly in the rain.
A distant figure stands under the streetlamp watching Qi Zhou's window.
Inside, Qi Zhou remains unaware of the figure outside.
A single drop falls into the iron basin.
The blue dream crystal gives one subtle physical tremble.
The radio announces tomorrow's work schedule in a calm bureaucratic voice.
Qi Zhou sits in silence beside the hidden secret.
The watcher is still outside, unmoving.
One final water drop lands in the basin; the room sound falls away.
Cut to black with only the last radio static lingering.
